direct_signals_from_project_papers: The UPV-SDG paper identifies SDG7, SDG9 and SDG12 as the strongest UPV profile and explicitly mentions sustainable mobility; the Larosa paper highlights energy transition plus mitigation and adaptation concerns.
inferred_upv_applied_families: renewable energy systems; power systems; smart grids; industrial decarbonization; low-carbon materials; sustainable mobility; zero-emission vehicles; carbon capture
non_activation_rule: Energy, mobility, industry or materials terms do not activate PB1 unless they co-occur with explicit emissions, carbon, warming, mitigation or climate-impact language.

direct_signals_from_project_papers: The PB-SDG paper repeatedly discusses ocean acidification together with marine ecosystems and SDG14.
inferred_upv_applied_families: marine monitoring; coastal water assessment; shellfish aquaculture; seawater sensors
non_activation_rule: Marine or coastal terms do not activate PB2 unless acidification, pH decline, carbonate chemistry or calcification effects are explicit.

direct_signals_from_project_papers: The planning PDF explicitly says agriculture alone is insufficient and demands evidence about nutrient flows, fertilization, contamination or altered biogeochemical cycles; the PB-SDG paper defines PB4 through N and P cycles.

non_activation_rule: Agriculture, wastewater or circular-economy terms do not activate PB4 unless nutrient, nitrogen, phosphorus, runoff, eutrophication or cycle-disruption language is explicit.

direct_signals_from_project_papers: The PB-SDG paper defines PB5 through blue-water use and basin withdrawals, highlights land-food-water trade-offs and gives irrigation expansion as an explicit pressure example; the Larosa paper flags water security.

non_activation_rule: Irrigation or water-management terms do not activate PB5 unless withdrawals, depletion, scarcity, blue-water use or environmental-flow pressure are explicit.

direct_signals_from_project_papers: The PB-SDG paper mentions agricultural expansion, maize expansion, deforestation, biomass imports, forest management, urbanization and infrastructure pressure on land systems.

non_activation_rule: GIS, remote sensing, planning or infrastructure terms do not activate PB6 unless the abstract clearly states land-cover conversion, deforestation, habitat conversion or sealed-surface expansion.

direct_signals_from_project_papers: The PB-SDG paper links PB7 to biodiversity loss and habitat protection; the UPV-SDG paper explicitly mentions biodiversity protection of green spaces and a campus biodiversity inventory.
inferred_upv_applied_families: biodiversity inventory; habitat restoration; green infrastructure; ecological corridors; urban biodiversity; pollinator conservation
non_activation_rule: Restoration, planning or inventory terms do not activate PB7 unless biodiversity, species, habitats or ecosystem integrity are explicit.

non_activation_rule: Materials, waste or chemistry terms do not activate PB8 unless the abstract clearly identifies a novel, synthetic, engineered, persistent or toxic substance.
